2605.23158.title: What Does the Server See? Understanding Privacy Leakage from Large Language Models in Split Inference
2605.23158.paper_summary: The deployment of large language models (LLMs) on resource-constrained devices remains challenging, spurring interest in split inference, where models are partitioned between client and server to reduce computational burden and enhance privacy by transmitting only intermediate activations. However, the privacy-preserving capabilities of split inference, particularly in the context of LLMs, have not been exhaustively investigated. To fill this gap, we introduce ActInv, which solves an intermediate activation matching problem to reconstruct the client's input. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that ActInv achieves high-fidelity reconstructions, even in the presence of common perturbation-based defenses such as Gaussian noise injection and activation sparsification. To systematically understand this vulnerability, we develop Perturbation Amplification Factor (PAF), a metric for quantifying a layer's inherent resistance to reconstruction. Our analysis reveals that privacy vulnerability is not uniform across layers, with some layers being highly susceptible to leakage while others offer natural resistance. Furthermore, we demonstrate that defense effectiveness can be significantly improved by calibrating perturbation directions to maximize reconstruction error during backpropagation. Building on these insights, we design PriPert and conduct comprehensive evaluations, covering privacy, utility, and computational overhead, to demonstrate its effectiveness.
2605.23158.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在資源受限的設備上部署仍然具有挑戰性，這引發了對分割推斷的興趣，該方法將模型分割在客戶端和伺服器之間，以減少計算負擔並通過僅傳輸中間激活來增強隱私。然而，分割推斷的隱私保護能力，特別是在LLMs的背景下，尚未得到充分研究。為填補這一空白，我們引入了ActInv，該方法解決了一個中間激活匹配問題，以重建客戶端的輸入。廣泛的評估顯示，ActInv即使在存在常見的基於擾動的防禦（如高斯噪聲注入和激活稀疏化）的情況下，也能實現高保真重建。為了系統性地理解這一脆弱性，我們開發了擾動放大因子（PAF），這是一個量化層固有抵抗重建能力的指標。我們的分析顯示，隱私脆弱性在不同層之間並不均勻，有些層對洩漏高度敏感，而其他層則具有自然的抵抗力。此外，我們展示了通過校準擾動方向以最大化反向傳播過程中的重建誤差，可以顯著提高防禦效果。在這些見解的基礎上，我們設計了PriPert並進行了全面評估，涵蓋隱私、效用和計算開銷，以證明其有效性。
2605.23158.author: Mingyuan Fan et.al.
2605.23158.authors: Mingyuan Fan, Yu Liu, Fuyi Wang, Cen Chen
